title: veronica fruticulosa subsp. saxatilis
intro: Veronica fruticulosa subsp. saxatilis, also known as Rock Speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rock Speedwell include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Rock Speedwell is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal infusions or added to salves for topical application.
